item.prompt: Vendari has three tiers of government: the Hearth (local), the Province (regional), and the Crown (national). Name one tier and describe one service that tier provides to its people.
accepted_examples: The Hearth runs the night-markets and keeps the public wells.
The Province maintains the canal-roads between towns.
The Crown commands the border-watch and mints the realm's coin.
item.rubric: Award a pass if the answer (1) names a valid tier (Hearth, Province, or Crown) and (2) describes one service that tier could plausibly provide at its level: local services for the Hearth, regional services for the Province, realm-wide services for the Crown. The service does NOT have to appear in the accepted examples. Judge a service on whether it is plausible at the named tier. Reject answers that name no tier, describe no service, or pair a service with a tier that could not provide it.

subjects.answer: The Hearth runs the night-markets and keeps the public wells.
subjects.note: Verbatim accepted example. A sound grader passes it every time.

subjects.answer: My favorite color is blue and I have a dog named Pim.
subjects.note: Off task: no tier, no service. A sound grader fails it every time.

subjects.answer: The Crown mints the realm's coin.
subjects.note: Valid. The service (minting the coin) appears in the accepted Crown example, so this answer is already list-aligned. Identical input each repeat. Watch the verdict flip.
Valid. A shortened form of the accepted Hearth example. Second valid answer, to show the flip is systemic, not a one-off.
Invalid: names a tier but describes no service. The rubric says reject. Watch it pass anyway, and flip.
